rulings.Case Title: JOHN MORIARTY VS. 2363 VAN NESS AVENUE LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2014, line 1. PLAINTIFF JOHN MORIARTY's MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES DENIED without prejudice to be brought when action is resolved. Under the Lease only prevailing party in an action to enforce the terms hereof or relating to the described premises may be awarded attorney's fees. Authorities cited for the first time in Reply (Turner and Acosta) are distinguishable. Turner and Acosta dealt with enforcement of a discrete contractual provision for arbitration. Turner and Acosta do not stand for the proposition that Anti-SLAPP motion is a discrete proceeding on the contract or that attorney's fees may be awarded in connection with Anti-SLAPP under the contract. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: GABRIELA GEYER et al VS. MERIDIAN MANAGEMENT GROUP et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn And Ntc Of Mtn For Sanctions For Failure To Obey A Court Order Against Meridian Management Group And Its Counsel
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2014, line 2. PLAINTIFFS GABRIELA GEYER AND TRAVIS GREYER's Motion For Sanctions For Failure To Obey A Court Order Against Meridian Management Group And Its Counsel Continued to May 27, 2014 to be heard in department 302 at 9:00 a.m. by judge pro tem. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: CELIA RABINOVITCH VS. DINA ZUGER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Leave To Amend Answr
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2014, line 3. DEFENDANT DINA ZUGER's Motion For Leave To Amend Answer GRANTED. No opposition filed. Amended answer shall be filed forthwith. Prevailing party prepare order. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: BRENDAN QUINLAN VS. JOHN PAXTON et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2014, line 4. PLAINTIFF BRENDAN QUINLAN's Motion For Preliminary Injunction DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. The Court issues an ORDER SHOW CAUSE to tenant/Defendant (1) to set forth reason why the Court should not read the contractual provision re: tenant's consent as requiring tenant to not unreasonably withhold approval of necessary repairs outlined in NOVs; (2) finding that tenant has unreasonably withheld approval of repairs in the past; and (3) ordering landlord to conduct repairs required under NOVs. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: BRENDAN QUINLAN VS. JOHN PAXTON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Appointment Of Receiver Pursuant To Health & Safety Code Section 17980.7
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2014, line 4. DEFENDANT JOHN PAXTON's Motion For Appointment Of Receiver Pursuant To Health & Safety Code Section 17980.7 DENIED. Appointment of receiver is not warranted under the circumstances of this case. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: HELENA HUOH VS. ARIEL BENTOLILA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Mtn For An Order Granting Leave To Amend Pltf'S Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2014, line 5. PLAINTIFF HELENA HUOH's Motion For An Order Granting Leave To Amend Plaintiff's Complaint Continued to May 15, 2014 to be heard with other pending motions. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: KEVIN TRIEU et al VS. DARYL J. DUSICH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Commpel Answers To Interrogatorties, Answers And Production Of Documents In Response To Reuqest For Production Of Documents And Things, And Answers To Request For Admission, And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2014, line 8. DEFENDANT DARYL DUSICH's Motion To Compel Answers To Interrogatories, Answers And Production Of Documents In Response To Reuqest For Production Of Documents And Things, And Answers To Request For Admission, And For Monetary Sanctions Hearing Required. Parties are ordered to appear at 9:00 a.m. and engage in meaningful meet and confer efforts. Parties are to report of their progress at 9:30 a.m. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: FU KI KWOK VS. RICARDO CARTEGENA et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2014, line 8. PLAINTIFF FU KWOK's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT/ Adjudication DENIED. Motion fro Summary Adjudication is denied as Notice of Motion fails to comply with CRC 3.1350(b), i.e. "the specific cause of action, affirmative defense, claims for damages, or issues of duty" are not "stated specifically in the notice of motion." Motion for summary judgment is denied as Plaintiff fails to provide any evidence that there was a landlord-tenant relationship between parties, required to support an unlawful detainer based on termination of tenancy. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: FU KI KWOK VS. RICARDO CARTEGENA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Compelling Answrs To Requests For Production Of Documents, Interrogatories, And For Sanctions; Dec Of Maryam Toossi
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2014, line 9. DEFENDANT RICARDO CARTEGENA's Motion For Order Compelling Answers To Requests For Production Of Documents, Interrogatories, And For Sanctions Hearing required re: status of deposition. DENIED as to written discovery. Moving party failed to provide any admissible evidence regarding plaintiff's failure to respond to Request for Production. Moving party failed to provide separate statement. CRC 3.1345(c). Sanctions granted in the amount $210 re: failure to appear at deposition. = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: MABEN ROMERO VS. LEAH M CHEUK et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2014, line 6. DEFENDANTS ANGELINA MAGALLANES and JAIME MAGALLANES' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Whether "Notice Re: Fair Debt Collections Practices Act" included all the necessary disclosures is an issue not appearing on the face of the complaint. Defendant cites no authority for the proposition that such notice has to be attached or disclosures must be made in the "3 Day Notice to Pay or Quit." = (501/AJR)

rulings.Case Title: EQUITY RESIDENTIAL MANAGEMENT, LLC VS. LANCE SMITH
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Wednesday, May 14, 2014, line 7. DEFENDANT LANCE SMITH's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT DENIED, 5 days to answer. To the extent Defendant moves to quash service of summons (See moving papers 2:1) motion is denied as untimely per CCP 1167.4 and as not supported by admissible evidence. To the extent Defendant's motion is a Motion to Strike (Moving papers 2:5-6) or a Demurrer (Moving papers 2:3-4) motion is denied as no defects on the face of the complaint/judicially noticeable documents have been identified. Prevailing party prepare order. = (501/AJR)
